brief:
# UX Research Brief: Bookmark List Screen
**QuickMarks — Screen 1 (Home / `/`)**
**Version:** 1.0 | **Prepared by:** UX Research Agent

---

## 1. Executive Summary

The Bookmark List screen is the primary surface of QuickMarks — a local-first, single-user bookmark manager. It must simultaneously serve as a discovery interface, a navigation hub, and a lightweight data management tool. This brief synthesises user needs, accessibility requirements, information architecture, and interaction patterns to guide the design and implementation of this screen.

---

## 2. User Needs Analysis

### 2.1 Primary Jobs-to-be-Done

| Priority | Job | Trigger | Success Condition |
|----------|-----|---------|-------------------|
| P0 | Find a specific bookmark quickly | "I need that link I saved last week" | User locates bookmark in < 10 seconds via search |
| P0 | Browse by topic/category | "Show me everything tagged `frontend`" | Multi-tag filter returns accurate, instant results |
| P1 | Save a new bookmark | "I need to capture this link now" | FAB is always visible; one tap to navigate to add form |
| P1 | Manage existing bookmarks | "This link is outdated, I need to delete it" | Edit and delete actions are accessible without extra navigation |
| P2 | Understand the collection at a glance | "What have I saved recently?" | Card metadata (title, tags, recency) communicates context without opening |

### 2.2 User Mental Models

- **Users expect search to be forgiving** — case-insensitive substring matching aligns with how users recall partial titles or domains.
- **Tags are categories, not metadata** — users treat tag chips as toggleable filters, not passive labels. The AND logic serves power users who narrow by intersection; "All" serves casual users who want a reset.
- **Recency matters for retrieval** — relative timestamps ("2h ago") support episodic memory ("I saved this today").
- **Deletion is high-stakes** — users need friction before permanent actions, but modal interruption feels disproportionate for a single card. Inline confirmation respects context.

### 2.3 User Pain Points to Avoid

- **False empty states** — distinguishing "no bookmarks exist" from "no bookmarks match filters" is critical; confusing them erodes trust in the tool.
- **Filter confusion** — if AND logic is not visually communicated, users will misinterpret zero results as a bug.
- **Action icon discoverability on desktop** — hiding edit/delete until hover risks users not discovering management functions exist.

---

## 3. Information Architecture

### 3.1 Screen Hierarchy

```
Bookmark List (/)
├── Filter Bar [sticky/top]
│   ├── SearchBar
│   │   └── Text input (real-time, case-insensitive, title + url)
│   └── TagFilterChips [horizontally scrollable]
│       ├── "All" chip (clears all active filters)
│       └── [Tag chip] × N (derived from BookmarkStore.getAllTags())
│
├── Content Area [scrollable]
│   ├── STATE: Has bookmarks + filters match
│   │   └── BookmarkCard Grid (1–3 columns, responsive)
│   │       └── BookmarkCard × N
│   │           ├── Title (truncated, 60 chars, ellipsis)
│   │           ├── URL (truncated, muted secondary)
│   │           ├── Tags (max 3 chips + "+N" overflow)
│   │           ├── Created At (relative time)
│   │           └── Actions [hover-revealed on desktop]
│   │               ├── Edit → /edit/:id
│   │               └── Delete → inline confirmation (card flip)
│   │
│   ├── STATE: No bookmarks exist
│   │   └── EmptyState
│   │       ├── Illustration placeholder
│   │       ├── Heading: "No bookmarks yet"
│   │       ├── Subtext: "Save your first link"
│   │       └── CTA Button → /add
│   │
│   └── STATE: Bookmarks exist, filters yield 0 matches
│       └── NoResultsState
│           ├── Message: "No bookmarks match your filters"
│           └── "Clear filters" text button (resets search + tags)
│
└── FAB [fixed, bottom-right]
    └── "+" → /add
```

### 3.2 Content Priority Order (per card)

1. **Title** — primary identification signal; largest, boldest text
2. **Tags** — categorical context; scannable at glance
3. **URL** — secondary identification; muted, not visually competing
4. **Created At** — temporal context; lowest visual weight
5. **Actions** — present but not dominant at rest

### 3.3 Filter Logic Architecture

```
Raw bookmark list (BookmarkStore.getAll())
        ↓
[Step 1] Tag filter applied (AND logic)
  — bookmark must include ALL active tag chips
  — if no chips active (or "All" selected): pass-through
        ↓
[Step 2] Search filter applied (substring match)
  — case-insensitive match on title OR url
  — if search string is empty: pass-through
        ↓
Rendered card set
```

> **Design implication:** Filter application order means tag filtering narrows the pool before search narrows further. This should be visually communicable — consider a subtle result count indicator ("5 of 12") to help users understand the active filter state.

---

## 4. Interaction Patterns

### 4.1 Real-Time Search

| Attribute | Specification |
|-----------|--------------|
| Trigger | `onChange` on every keystroke |
| Debounce | None required (client-side, in-memory) |
| Scope | `title` (substring) + `url` (substring) |
| Case sensitivity | Insensitive |
| Clear affordance | "×" clear button appears when input is non-empty |
| Empty input behaviour | Shows full tag-filtered set (no results hidden) |

**Accessibility consideration:** Search results update should be announced via a live region. A visually hidden `aria-live="polite"` element should announce result count changes (e.g., "3 bookmarks found").

### 4.2 Tag Filter Chips

| State | Visual Treatment | Token Reference |
|-------|-----------------|-----------------|
| Inactive | Outlined style, `border-default`, `text-secondary` | `ink-100` border, `ink-600` text |
| Active | Filled style, `cta-primary` background, `text-on-cta` | `indigo-500` bg, `#FFFFFF` text |
| "All" chip | Special: always visible, clears all active tags | Outlined until filters active; filled when active |
| Hover | `hover_opacity: 0.08` overlay on chip surface | 150ms ease transition |

**Pattern detail:**
- "All" chip should be pinned to the left of the scrollable row, non-scrollable if possible, as it is a control action, not a filter value.
- Horizontal scrolling row requires visible scroll affordance on desktop (fade-out gradient at right edge) and touch-friendly scrolling on mobile (no visible scrollbar).
- Active chip count badge or label ("2 tags active") aids AND-logic comprehension.

### 4.3 BookmarkCard Hover / Focus State

```
At rest (desktop):
  ┌─────────────────────────────┐
  │ Title                  [··] │  ← actions hidden
  │ url.com                     │
  │ [tag1] [tag2] +1  · 2h ago │
  └─────────────────────────────┘
  Shadow: elevation level 1

On hover / focus (desktop):
  ┌─────────────────────────────┐
  │ Title              [✎] [🗑] │  ← actions revealed
  │ url.com                     │
  │ [tag1] [tag2] +1  · 2h ago │
  └─────────────────────────────┘
  Shadow: elevation level 2 (0 4px 12px …)
  Transition: 200ms ease-out

Mobile (always visible):
  Actions rendered as persistent icon buttons below card content
```

**Keyboard pattern:** `Tab` moves focus to card; `Enter` navigates to detail (if Screen 3 exists) or edit. Arrow keys do not navigate between cards (standard tab flow preferred for simplicity). Within card, `Tab` reaches edit and delete action buttons.

### 4.4 Inline Delete Confirmation (Card Flip)

```
Trigger: User clicks trash icon
         ↓
Card face flips (200ms crossfade) to confirmation state:
  ┌─────────────────────────────┐
  │  Delete this bookmark?      │
  │                             │
  │  [Cancel]  [Confirm Delete] │
  └─────────────────────────────┘

Auto-revert: 5 seconds with no action → crossfades back to card content
Confirm: Card animates out (fade + collapse), bookmark deleted from store
Cancel: Crossfades back to card content immediately
```

**Accessibility considerations:**
- Focus must move to the "Cancel" button immediately on flip (safer default).
- Focus must be trapped within the flipped card until action is taken or auto-revert occurs.
- `aria-live="assertive"` on the confirmation container to announce to screen readers.
- Auto-revert countdown should not be announced repeatedly; a single announcement on appearance is sufficient.

### 4.5 Empty State vs. No Results State

| Condition | Component | Copy | CTA |
|-----------|-----------|------|-----|
| `BookmarkStore.getAll()` returns `[]` | `EmptyState` | "No bookmarks yet" / "Save your first link" | Button → `/add` |
| Store has items but filtered set is `[]` | `NoResultsState` | "No bookmarks match your filters" | Text button: "Clear filters" |

**Design guidance:**
- `EmptyState` should use the brand illustration style (line-based, ink-100 + indigo-500 accent, high negative space). Suggested motif: abstract link chain or grid structure.
- `NoResultsState` should be text-only or use a minimal icon — no full illustration. Users know data exists; the illustration would be misleading.
- Both states should be vertically centred in the content area to feel intentional, not broken.

### 4.6 FAB (Floating Action Button)

| Attribute | Detail |
|-----------|--------|
| Position | Fixed, bottom-right. Offset: 24px from bottom, 24px from right edge |
| Size | Minimum 44×44px touch target (design token: `touch_targets.minimum_height`) |
| Icon | "+" (plus), white on `cta-primary` background |
| Elevation | Level 3 shadow to float above card grid |
| Scroll behaviour | Remains fixed; does not scroll with content |
| Overlap concern | On mobile 1-column layout, FAB must not obscure the last card's actions — consider 80px bottom padding on the card grid |

---

## 5. Accessibility Requirements

### 5.1 Keyboard Navigation Map

```
Tab order:
SearchBar input
  → TagFilterChips (each chip is focusable, Space/Enter toggles)
    → BookmarkCard 1
      → [Card action: Edit button]
      → [Card action: Delete button]
    → BookmarkCard 2 … N
      → FAB (last in tab order before wrapping)
```

### 5.2 ARIA Landmark Structure

```html
<header>           ← Filter bar (SearchBar + TagFilterChips)
<main>             ← Card grid or state component
<region aria-label="Bookmark filters">  ← TagFilterChips container
```

### 5.3 Component-Level ARIA Requirements

| Component | ARIA Requirements |
|-----------|------------------|
| SearchBar | `role="searchbox"`, `aria-label="Search bookmarks"`, result count announced via live region |
| TagFilterChip | `role="checkbox"`, `aria-checked="true/false"`, `aria-label="{tag name} filter"` |
| "All" chip | `role="button"`, `aria-label="Clear all tag filters"` |
| BookmarkCard | `role="article"`, `aria-label="{title}"` |
| Delete confirmation | `aria-live="assertive"`, focus trap, `role="group"` |
| FAB | `aria-label="Add new bookmark"` |
| EmptyState CTA | `aria-label="Save your first bookmark"` |
| Search live region | `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"`, visually hidden |
| Tag chips row | `role="group"`, `aria-label="Filter by tag"` |

### 5.4 Colour Contrast Checklist

| Element | Foreground | Background | Minimum Ratio |
|---------|-----------|------------|---------------|
| Card title | `ink-950` (#0D0F12) | `white` (#FFFFFF) | ≥ 4.5:1 ✓ |
| Card URL | `ink-600` (#3A414D) | `white` (#FFFFFF) | ≥ 4.5:1 — verify |
| Active chip text | `white` (#FFFFFF) | `indigo-500` (#4F6AFF) | ≥ 4.5:1 — verify |
| Inactive chip text | `ink-600` (#3A414D) | `ink-50` (#EEF1F5) | ≥ 4.5:1 — verify |
| Placeholder text | `ink-300` (#8C95A3) | `white` (#FFFFFF) | ≥ 3:1 (large text) |

> ⚠️ **Flag for implementation:** `ink-600` on `white` and active chip `white` on `indigo-500` should be contrast-checked programmatically during build. `amber-400` warning colour should never be used as the sole indicator — always pair with an icon or text label.

### 5.5 Motion & Reduced Motion

All transitions (card flip, chip toggle, FAB) must respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. When enabled:
- Card flip: instant swap, no crossfade
- Chip toggle: no transition, immediate state change
- Card hover elevation: no shadow transition

---

## 6. Responsive Design Considerations

### 6.1 Breakpoint Behaviour

| Breakpoint | Grid | Filter Bar | FAB Offset | Card Actions |
|-----------|------|-----------|------------|-------------|
| `< 768px` (mobile) | 1 column, 16px margin | Stacked: search above chips | 16px from edges | Always visible below card content |
| `768–1023px` (tablet) | 2 columns, 24px gutter | Inline: search + chips in same row | 24px from edges | Always visible (hover not reliable) |
| `≥ 1024px` (desktop) | 3 columns, 24px gutter | Inline | 24px from edges | Revealed on hover/focus |

### 6.2 Tag Chip Row Scroll on Mobile

- No visible scrollbar (use `overflow-x: auto; scrollbar-width: none`).
- Right-edge fade gradient (using a pseudo-element with a white-to-transparent gradient) signals overflow.
- Swipe gesture is native browser behaviour — no custom implementation needed.

### 6.3 Card Content Truncation Rules

| Field | Truncation Rule | Implementation |
|-------|----------------|----------------|
| Title | 60 chars, ellipsis | CSS `text-overflow: ellipsis`, single line, `overflow: hidden` |
| URL | Single line, ellipsis | Same CSS pattern |
| Tags | Max 3 chips visible | `+N` overflow chip counts remaining tags; tooltip on hover shows all tags |
| Notes | Not shown on card | Notes visible only in detail/edit screen |

---

## 7. State Management & Data Flow

### 7.1 Screen States Summary

```
BookmarkStore state ──────────────────────────────────┐
                                                       ▼
                                         isEmpty? → EmptyState
                                              ↓ No
                              Active filters? → Apply tag filter (AND)
                                              ↓
                               Apply search filter (substring)
                                              ↓
                              Results empty? → NoResultsState
                                              ↓ No
                                         Card Grid
```

### 7.2 Filter State Requirements

The filter state (active tag chips + search string) should be:
- **Ephemeral** — not persisted to `BookmarkStore`; resets on page load.
- **Coordinated** — clearing via "All" chip resets both tag and search states; "Clear filters" in NoResultsState resets both.
- **Non-destructive** — filter state does not modify bookmark data.

### 7.3 Derived Data

| Derived Value | Source | Notes |
|--------------|--------|-------|
| Tag chip list | `BookmarkStore.getAllTags()` | Deduplicated, alphabetically sorted. Recomputed when bookmarks are added/deleted. |
| Filtered card list | `getAll()` → tag filter → search filter | Recomputed on every filter state change |
| Tag overflow count (+N) | Per-card tag array length − 3 | Only computed for display; all tags remain filterable |
| Relative timestamp | `createdAt` ISO 8601 | Formatted client-side at render time; does not auto-update during session |

---

## 8. Design Tokens Applied to This Screen

### 8.1 Surface & Elevation

| Element | Token |
|---------|-------|
| Page background | `background-primary` (`ink-0` / `#F8F9FB`) |
| Card at rest | `surface-primary` (`white`) + elevation level 1 |
| Card on hover | `surface-primary` + elevation level 2 |
| Filter bar background | `surface-primary` or `surface-secondary` depending on scroll shadow implementation |
| FAB | `cta-primary` (`indigo-500`) + elevation level 3 |

### 8.2 Typography Scale

| Element | Role | Size / Weight |
|---------|------|--------------|
| Card title | `body` or `label` (bold override) | 14px / 500 |
| Card URL | `label` | 12px / 500, `text-secondary` |
| Card tags | `small` | 11px / 400 |
| Card timestamp | `small` | 11px / 400, `text-disabled` |
| EmptyState heading | `heading-2` | 24px / 700 |
| EmptyState subtext | `body` | 14px / 400, `text-secondary` |
| NoResults message | `body` | 14px / 400 |

### 8.3 Motion Tokens

| Interaction | Duration Token | Easing |
|-------------|---------------|--------|
| Chip toggle | `fast` (100ms) | `ease-out` |
| Card hover elevation | `normal` (200ms) | `ease-out` |
| Delete card flip | `normal` (200ms) | `ease-out` |
| FAB appearance (if animated) | `normal` (200ms) | `emphasized` |

---

## 9. Edge Cases & Design Decisions

### 9.1 Identified Edge Cases

| Edge Case | Expected Behaviour |
|-----------|-------------------|
| Very long URL with no spaces | Truncated with ellipsis; full URL visible on hover tooltip or detail screen |
| Tag with 20 characters | Renders at full width in chip; may wrap in narrow containers — test at mobile breakpoint |
| 5 tags on a card | 3 visible + "+2" overflow chip |
| Single bookmark in store | "All" chip still appears (only one tag chip); grid renders as 1 card |
| Tag that exists only on deleted bookmarks | `getAllTags()` is recomputed; chip disappears from filter row. No orphan chips. |
| Active filter on a tag from a deleted bookmark | Filter is cleared automatically when tag chip disappears on recompute |
| Search matches URL, not title | Card correctly surfaces (both fields searched); URL highlighted if implementation supports it |
| All bookmarks deleted via bulk delete (future) | Screen transitions to EmptyState |
| Store returns 100+ bookmarks | No pagination defined in PRD — consider virtual scrolling as a performance consideration for future iteration |

### 9.2 Explicit Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| AND logic for tag filters | Power-user behaviour; OR logic would return too many results for multi-tag selection and dilute the filtering utility |
| No modal for delete | Inline card flip maintains spatial context; modals interrupt flow disproportionately for a single-item action |
| Action icons hidden on desktop at rest | Reduces visual noise on dense grids; hover/focus reveal is a standard affordance. Desktop users have persistent cursor; mobile users get always-visible actions |
| Filter order: tags first, then search | Tags narrow a larger set; search precision is highest-value as a final filter. Reversing this order would produce identical results but tags-first is semantically cleaner |
| "All" chip clears both tags AND search | Users expect "All" to mean "show everything" — partial reset would be confusing |
| Relative timestamps, not absolute | Supports episodic recall ("I saved this today") for a recency-driven retrieval pattern |

---

## 10. Open Questions for Design Review

1. **Result count indicator:** Should a "Showing N of M bookmarks" label appear below the filter bar? This would directly address AND-logic confusion but adds visual weight to the filter bar.

2. **Sort order:** The PRD does not specify default card sort order. Recommendation: `createdAt` descending (newest first) as the most useful default for retrieval. Should this be user-configurable in a future iteration?

3. **Tag chip "All" positioning:** Should "All" be pinned (non-scrollable) or scroll with the rest of the chips? Pinning it is recommended but requires a split layout for the chip row.

4. **Hover tooltip for +N tag overflow:** Should hovering the "+2" chip show all tag names in a tooltip? This improves discoverability without requiring navigation to the detail screen.

5. **Search scope expansion:** Should search eventually include `notes` field content? Currently scoped to `title` and `url` only per PRD — but notes contain meaningful retrieval keywords.

6. **Auto-revert countdown on delete confirmation:** Should the 5-second auto-revert be visually indicated (e.g., a subtle progress indicator on the card border)? This would make the timing transparent to the user.

7. **Keyboard shortcut for search:** A `/` or `Ctrl+F` shortcut to focus the SearchBar would significantly improve power-user efficiency. Out of scope for v1.0 but worth flagging.

---

## 11. Success Metrics

| Metric | Target | Measurement Method |
|--------|--------|-------------------|
| Time to find a specific bookmark via search | < 10 seconds from page load | Usability test task timing |
| Tag filter comprehension (AND logic) | Users correctly predict results before applying second tag | Think-aloud usability session |
| Delete action discoverability (desktop) | > 80% of users find delete within 30 seconds without prompting | First-click test |
| Empty state vs. no-results state differentiation | 0 user confusion incidents in moderated testing | Observation + verbal protocol |
| Filter clear action success | Users successfully reset all filters in 1 action | Task completion rate |

---

*Brief prepared for design handoff and frontend implementation. Cross-reference with Screen 2 (Add/Edit Bookmark) brief for form interaction patterns and navigation continuity.*
